but you didn't change the net income margin guidance
we have assumed the tariffs as they are right now in our expectations, right? And so we are absorbing quite a bit of that expense, and that all sits in the four-wall perspective
with respect to the longer term, we'll see where the tariffs play out and we'll adapt as we always have
I realize COGS were a helper with that flat food cost

that was affected by the LA fires and the weather impact was a little greater, just given our concentration in Texas

being able to triangulate between the weather, the fires, the holiday shifts, the retail pull forward in March, you know, that's a lot to parse out over the first four months
how same-store sales progress through the quarter and any color you could give on, you know, exit rate in April
obviously we finished near the high end, but it wasn't at the high end of our guide
the environment overall feels not quite as robust as we expected. And then, you know, all of the news and all of the economists are saying expect a 1% or so impact in the back half of the year
I would continue to expect the mix to be negative for us. I don't know the exact number, but that 1.5% to 2% range for modeling purposes is probably pretty good.
We've talked a lot about the dip in alcohol sales, but if you really go back and look at the past four quarters now, it's not like that's taken another step down.
Cheesecake Factory was at 22% total off-prem, which was exact same as Q1 of 2024 and up 1% sequentially from last quarter.

we're not really anticipating any significant tariff impacts to construction costs, which have, by the way, come back really nicely in line with our long-term expectations following the COVID inflation
certainly we continue to target that 25 openings this year. We'll target for the first half, 16. So I think that that's a really good cadence for us. And so we're ahead of schedule.
our portfolio of brands continue to attract landlords that want experiential dining in their projects
If you look at dessert sales for Cheesecake Factory, they're still sitting at 17% of total sales.
They all comp positive in the first quarter in all those different geographies. So the brand continues to resonate everywhere around the world.
Historically, casual dining demand has had more macro sensitivity versus kind of the QSRs. But some recent surveys seem to suggest that it is actually holding up relatively better this time around.
Whereas our cohort, the income, it depends on the slowdown, but incomes aren't slowing down. income of 100 plus K, those jobs are going away. There's not mass layoffs.
If you look at percentage of wallet share over time, it has grown.
The retention in the restaurants at the management level and the staff level continues to be incredibly consistent over the past six quarters now.
there has been a transition over time to heavier labor because of the wages and probably a lower COGS in the industry in general, certainly for us
we'll always focus on bringing new items in at very attractive price points to offset the need to take up pricing
we'll continue to lean into menu innovation and be able to put on what we believe people want to eat today

notes: Chunk contains a single event: part 2 of 2 of the CAKE 2025-04-30 earnings call transcript (Q&A section only); no fiscal_kpis array in this chunk.
All candidates come from the one transcript event; dedup applied within chunk (e.g. commodity inflation folded into food_cost; spring-break timing folded into holiday_timing).

Skipped as vague/banned/duplicative (10): March retail pull-forward (change-noun, instance-bound), anti-American sentiment/geopolitics (sentiment word, refuted by management), macro labor force (vague), vegetarian/Mediterranean menu trend (folded into menu_innovation), April exit rate (period-bound), spring break timing (folded into holiday_timing), fresh-ingredient/commissary-bypass structural advantage (company attribute, not reusable cause), commodity inflation (duplicate of food_cost), CapEx trajectory (folded into construction_cost/unit_development), day-part/day-of-week consistency (no causal variable).
Brand-level drivers (north_italia_*) kept separate from company-wide forms per ontology R9.
wildfire named at class level per R10 (LA fires = instance of reusable wildfire class).
